session.assistant: Now let me check if there's a post-plan-implement CI hook:
session.tool_result:
1→# Post-Implementation CI 2→ 3→Run CI validation after plan implementation using `make fast-ci`. 4→ 5→Load the `ci-iteration` skill for the iterative fix workflow. 6→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
session.assistant: I need to run `make fast-ci`. Let me use the devrun agent:

tool_use.name: Task
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` and report the results. Just run the command and report what happens — do not modify any files.

session.tool_result:
## Results: make fast-ci
**Status: FAILED (exit code 2)**
### Summary
The `make fast-ci` command failed due to **Prettier code style issues**, but all other checks and tests passed.
### Breakdown
**Checks that passed:** - Lint (ruff): All checks passed - Format Check (ruff): All 1675 files already formatted - Markdown Check: OK - Exec Reference Check: OK (.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md is up to date) - Type Check (ty): All checks passed - Unit Tests (erk): 5819 passed in 23.38s - Unit Tests (erk-dev): 136 passed in 4.57s - Unit Tests (erk-statusline): 115 passed in 0.88s - Unit Tests (erk-mcp): 24 passed
**Issues found:**
Prettier found code style issues in 4 files: 1. docs/learned/architecture/remote-github-gateway.md 2. docs/learned/cli/checkout-teleport-split.md 3. docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md 4. docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md
The error message indicates: "Code style issues found in 4 files. Run Prettier with --write to fix."
... [3 lines omitted] ...
All functional tests and type checks pass successfully. The failure is solely due to formatting issues in markdown documentation files.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! The `make fast-ci` command completed successfully. Here's a summary of the results:
**make fast-ci: PASSED**
All checks passed:

The entire fast CI suite ran successfully with no errors or failures. agentId: ae73a21 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. PR #8937 submitted: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8937
## Summary of changes
**Updated existing files:** - `docs/learned/erk/workspace-activation.md` — Removed stale `uv pip install --no-deps` references, updated to document `uv sync --quiet` (Step 1-2) - `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/check_cmd.py` — Updated `validate_objective()` docstring to include Check 8 (roadmap table sync) and updated `check_objective()` brief description (Step 3) - `docs/learned/testing/testing.md` — Added test helper default parameter exemption section (Step 9)
**Created new documentation:** - `docs/learned/cli/checkout-teleport-split.md` — Checkout vs teleport design, cmux-open-pr `--mode` flag (Step 4) - `docs/learned/architecture/remote-github-gateway.md` — RemoteGitHub ABC (17 methods), repo_resolution utilities, remote vs local mode (Step 5) - `docs/learned/testing/test-layer-migration.md` — When/how to move tests between unit and integration layers (Step 6) - `docs/learned/ci/uv-cache-management.md` — UV cache pruning disabled for ephemeral CI runners (Step 7) - `docs/learned/commands/skill-patterns.md` — AskUserQuestion pattern for user-facing decisions, prompt_executor naming (Step 10)
**Auto-regenerated** (via `erk docs sync`): 12 index and tripwire files updated with new entries.
